commit.message:
Add unified ServerDiagnostics page combining LogMonitor and ModIssues

Create a new tabbed diagnostics page that merges the features of
LogMonitorPage.jsx and ModIssues.jsx into a single interface:

- Header with SERVER DIAGNOSTICS title and refresh button
- Summary stats bar: Total Errors, Critical, Active Mod Issues,
  Active Alerts, Mods With Issues
- Errors Per Day stacked bar chart (recharts)
- Tab 1 (Live Errors): occurrences table with filters and pagination
- Tab 2 (Error Patterns): error type catalog with detail dialog
- Tab 3 (Mod Analysis): mod issue cards with confidence scoring,
  verdict system, and full detail dialog
- Tab 4 (Alerts & Incidents): active alerts with resolve action
  and cross-link to mod issues

Follows existing style conventions (gold accent, dark theme, shadcn
components). Existing LogMonitorPage.jsx and ModIssues.jsx are
left untouched.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Dashboard view for landing state (#55)

* feat: add Dashboard view to landing state

Card grid showing all sessions with health color bars (green/amber/red
by review score), key metrics (duration, cost, errors, autonomy), and
a summary stats bar across the top. Discovered-only sessions appear
dimmed with "Not yet analyzed".

Toggle between List and Dashboard persists to localStorage. Dashboard
expands to 1100px wide to accommodate the grid. Filters (format, sort)
and search also persist.

* fix: dashboard visibility with all-discovered sessions

- Remove 0.7 opacity on discovered cards; use bg.base instead of bg.surface
- Bump text colors (muted -> secondary) so cards are readable on dark bg
- Stats bar always shows when there are sessions; nulls display as '--'
- Change 'Not yet analyzed' to 'Open to analyze'
- Show discovered count in sessions stat card

* Refine landing dashboard and demo screenshots

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Cursor/development environment setup 3671 (#133)

* Add Cursor Cloud specific instructions for Linux development

- Document Linux-specific environment setup (GPU acceleration, node-pty rebuild, spawn-helper compilation)
- Document dev mode race condition workaround
- Note cr-sqlite and computer-use feature limitations on Linux
- Add tips for running Electron app and tests efficiently

Co-authored-by: Arul Sharma <arul28@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix cr-sqlite sync API to gracefully degrade on Linux

The sync API methods (getDbVersion, exportChangesSince, applyChanges)
threw when cr-sqlite extension was unavailable, preventing project
initialization on platforms without the extension (Linux).

Changed to return safe no-op defaults (0, [], empty result) instead
of throwing, allowing the sync service to initialize harmlessly
without CRDT sync capability.

Co-authored-by: Arul Sharma <arul28@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove accidentally committed .ade db backup files

Co-authored-by: Arul Sharma <arul28@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix onboarding to trigger on new machines with tracked .ade directory

Check for ade.db (gitignored, machine-local) instead of the .ade
directory (tracked in git with shared config). This ensures onboarding
runs when a project is cloned to a new machine that has the shared
scaffold but no local runtime state.

Co-authored-by: Arul Sharma <arul28@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add env var fallback for GitHub, Linear, and AI API key credentials

Allows headless/CI/cloud environments to provide credentials via
environment variables when Electron's safeStorage encrypted store
is empty or unavailable.

- GitHub: GITHUB_TOKEN or ADE_GITHUB_TOKEN
- Linear: LINEAR_API_KEY or ADE_LINEAR_TOKEN
- AI providers: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, GOOGLE_API_KEY,
  MISTRAL_API_KEY, DEEPSEEK_API_KEY, XAI_API_KEY, GROQ_API_KEY,
  TOGETHER_API_KEY, OPENROUTER_API_KEY

The encrypted store remains the primary source. Env vars are only
checked as a fallback when no stored credential is found.

Co-authored-by: Arul Sharma <arul28@users.noreply.github.com>

* Auto-complete onboarding when env var credentials are detected

When a project is opened for the first time on a new machine and
environment variables provide at least one credential (GitHub token,
AI API key, or Linear token), mark onboarding as complete so the
wizard is skipped. This lets cloud agents and CI environments that
set credentials via env vars go straight to the Work view.

Co-authored-by: Arul Sharma <arul28@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: expand AI provider env var check in onboarding auto-complete to cover all 9 supported providers

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/arul28/ADE/sessions/e1b5e26f-5a60-433c-8c20-80be8492a456

Co-authored-by: arul28 <31745423+arul28@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add interactive mode to background preset switcher

- Enable interactive selection when no argument is provided

- Auto-detect all preset-* directories and display numbered list

- Maintain backward compatibility with direct argument mode

- Improve user feedback with enhanced output messages

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Code review fixes, shutdown cleanup, SDK upgrade (#51)

* Fix all code review findings: bugs, test hygiene, dead code, docs

Critical:
- qaAgent.test.js: add vi.resetModules() for fresh module state per test
- qaAgent.test.js: replace setTimeout(5) with queueMicrotask to eliminate flaky risk

Warnings:
- useDiscoveredSessions: wrap fetchSessionContent in useCallback for stable refs
- sessionLibrary: export pruneDeadEntries(), use in App.jsx onRefresh instead of
  direct localStorage write
- App.jsx: fix allSessions error fallback to return filtered entries
- InboxView: tie refresh spinner to Promise completion instead of fixed 800ms timer
- Add tests for clipToLength, pruneDeadEntries, transformedContent fallback

Nits:
- Remove dead entry.path references in InboxView and App.jsx
- Rename truncatePreviewText to clipToLength with JSDoc
- Extract StatsView IIFE to pre-computed modelUsageData above the return
- Consolidate 3 duplicate truncate helpers in parsers to shared import
- Add theme.d.ts and formatTime.d.ts to file trees in CLAUDE.md and README.md
- Move test-files/ to src/__tests__/fixtures/ (colocated with tests)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix slow Ctrl+C: graceful SDK shutdown + force-exit timeout

server.close() blocked waiting for open SSE connections and the warm
Copilot SDK subprocess. Added:
- shutdownQA() in qaAgent.js to stop the warm CopilotClient subprocess
- server close handler now ends SSE clients and calls shutdownQA()
- 500ms force-exit timeout in agentviz.js SIGINT/SIGTERM handler as
  a safety net when npm signal propagation stalls

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Upgrade @github/copilot-sdk 0.2.0 -> 0.2.1

Bugfix release: ephemeral event handling fix, env var consistency.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add Shadow Lift preset using power-curve gamma algorithm

Implements the shadow lift algorithm with gamma=1.5 (exponent≈0.667).
The power curve raises dark tones more than bright tones while keeping
pure white anchored. Max deviation from identity is ~9,800 at i≈75,
well within the 32,000 driver safety limit.

commit.message:
fix: use absolute import for nl_to_sparql in function_app.py

Relative imports (from .nl_to_sparql) fail in Azure Functions v2
because the api/ directory is not treated as a Python package.
The function app root is added to sys.path, so absolute imports work.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Prove znz_p_field: Z/pZ forms a field for prime p

- Add znz_field_inv_val: epsilon-based inverse for Z/pZ
- Add znz_field_inv_spec: inverse correctness lemma
- Add znz_p_ring: Ring instance for Z/pZ
- Add znz_p_field: Field instance for Z/pZ (prime p)
- Update progress/field_progress.md: mark all complete
- Update proof_knowledge_base.md: add entries for new definitions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add natural language query endpoint (/api/ask)

Add NL-to-SPARQL translation using GitHub Models GPT-4o-mini with
schema-injected few-shot prompting. The /api/ask endpoint accepts
natural language questions, translates to SPARQL, validates syntax
via RDFLib, enforces safety constraints (block mutating queries,
inject LIMIT), caches results, and retries with error feedback.

- api/nl_to_sparql.py: translation module with prompt, validation, cache
- api/function_app.py: new /api/ask route alongside existing /api/sparql
- api/requirements.txt: add openai>=1.0
- tests/test_nl_to_sparql.py: 28 tests covering validation, safety, cache, LLM mock
- README.md: document /api/ask with curl/JS/Python examples

commit.message:
Prove count_multiples_in_range and prime_pow_coprime_distinct

Both Admitted lemmas in integer.v are now fully proven, making
euler_phi_three_prime_powers Admitted-free.

New sub-lemmas added before count_multiples_in_range:
- mod_add_mul_small: (p*m+r) mod p = r for r < p
- filter_false_forall: Forall (f x = false) l -> filter f l = []
- filter_window_single_multiple: filter (k mod p =? 0) (seq (p*m) p) = [p*m]

Proofs:
- count_multiples_in_range: induction on m, seq_app + filter_app + above
- prime_pow_coprime_distinct: 3-step via prime_rel_prime/Zis_gcd_gcd,
  Z.coprime_pow_l/r, Z.gcd_greatest/divide_1_r

Updated progress/euler_phi_progress.md and proof_knowledge_base.md.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add euler_phi definition and main theorem euler_phi_three_prime_powers

- euler_phi: Euler's totient function (counts k in {0,...,n-1} with gcd(k,n)=1)
- group_order_unique, group_order_iso, group_order_product: GroupOrder algebraic properties
- euler_phi_group_order: phi(n) = |units(Z/nZ)| via bijection
- znz_units_decomp2: 2-var CRT isomorphism (Z/pqZ)* ≅ (Z/pZ)* × (Z/qZ)*
- euler_phi_mul: multiplicativity phi(p*q) = phi(p)*phi(q) when gcd(p,q)=1
- prime_pow_coprime_iff: gcd(k,p^e)=1 ↔ k mod p ≠ 0 for prime p
- euler_phi_prime_pow: phi(p^e) = p^(e-1)*(p-1) for prime p, e≥1 (depends on Admitted count lemma)
- nat_gcd_mul_coprime: gcd(a,c)=1 ∧ gcd(b,c)=1 → gcd(a*b,c)=1 (Nat level, via Gauss)
- euler_phi_three_prime_powers: main theorem phi(p^e*q^f*r^g) = p^(e-1)(p-1)*q^(f-1)(q-1)*r^(g-1)(r-1)
  (depends on Admitted: count_multiples_in_range, prime_pow_coprime_distinct)

[paper] Fix Paper 10 formal results: downgrade Theorem 2 to Proposition, fill proof gap in near-spherical asymptotics

F1: Downgrade identifiability-lifting Theorem to Proposition

  derivation. Honest about this now.

  to prop:p10_lifting

  conclusion, cover letter, and reviewer suggestions

- Added Gram matrix A(e) = J~'J~ with even expansion
- Showed first-order eigenvalue perturbation vanishes because v0 in ker(J~0)
- Leading contribution at O(e^4) for mu3, hence sigma3 = O(e^2)

commit.message:
[paper] Add self-contained Ritz basis appendix to Paper 10

Reviewer D (MAJOR REVISION) identified that the Ritz model is central to
Paper 10 but its basis functions, boundary conditions, convergence, and
n=1 treatment were never stated. The paper depended entirely on companion
papers for these details.

New content:
- sections/appendix.tex with four subsections:
  A.1 Coordinate system and trial functions (oblate spheroidal coords,
      2-DOF Legendre ansatz for normal/tangential displacement)
  A.2 Energy functionals and eigenvalue problem (membrane, bending,
      prestress stiffness; shell inertia and fluid added mass)
  A.3 Free boundary conditions and rigid-body modes (n=1 treatment)
  A.4 Convergence of the Ritz basis (enriched basis study: N=1
      overestimates by 3.3%, converged by N=3)

Main text fixes:
- main.tex: \input{sections/appendix} added before bibliography
- background.tex: forward reference to Appendix A replaces 'does not
  repeat that derivation'
- theory.tex: forward reference at Ritz lifting theorem; finite-difference
  step delta=1e-6 noted after scaled Jacobian definition
- results.tex: J_s -> \widetilde{J} in two places for notation consistency
- README.md: updated Paper 10 draft link to new snapshot

Compiled: 30 pages, no errors.

commit.message:
[paper] P10: upgrade Lemma to Proposition with kernel-direction argument + gap figure

Upgrade the forward-inverse adequacy result from a Lemma to a Proposition
that explicitly states the structural mechanism:

  For any v in ker(D pi), the reduced model is exactly insensitive
  (J_sphere * v = 0) while the full model retains sensitivity
  (J_oblate * v != 0). Forward adequacy constrains function VALUES
  to be close; it places no constraint on DERIVATIVES along the
  lost direction.

Changes:
- theory.tex: Lemma -> Proposition with kernel-direction proof + Remark
  connecting to Corollary (kappa ~ eps^{-2} near sphere while forward
  error shrinks)
- introduction.tex: Lemma -> Proposition reference
- conclusion.tex: update formal-result level description
- cover-letter.tex: Lemma -> Proposition with mechanism description
- New figure: fig_forward_inverse_gap.pdf (twin-axis plot showing
  forward error on left axis vs condition numbers on right axis)
- New script: scripts/forward_inverse_gap.py
- Removed: theorem4_proof.tex, forward_inverse_proof.py (previous
  incorrect approach with mathematical errors)

Key numbers at canonical point (eps = 0.75, c/a = 0.667):
  E_fwd = 9.36%, kappa_oblate = 69.4, kappa_sphere = 1.37e10
  Gap ratio = 1.97e8
  J_sphere[:,a] / J_sphere[:,c] = 2.0 exactly (rank collapse)
  ||J_oblate * v_null|| = 1.10 (full model sees null direction)
  ||J_sphere * v_null|| = 2.2e-10 (reduced model blind)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
types: re-record range bound spans with inferred element type (#634)

For-loop range literals like `0..10` are synthesized with a fresh TypeVar
for the element type so the loop body can constrain it (e.g. from an
`i32` accumulator).  The *range* TypeVar was already unified correctly,
but the *bound literal spans* (the `0` and `10`) were permanently
recorded as `I64` by the initial `synthesize` call.

Because codegen uses `resolvedTypeOf(span)` in `generateLiteral` to
choose the MLIR constant width, this caused two issues:
1. Bounds were always emitted as i64 constants even when the range was
   inferred to be `Range<i32>`, producing unnecessarily wide IR.
2. A bound that doesn't fit in the inferred type (e.g. `0..300` with
   `i8` constraint) would silently generate a truncated constant —
   e.g. 300 → 44 — causing wrong iteration counts at runtime.

Fix:
- Add `deferred_range_bounds: Vec<(Span, TypeVar, Option<i64>)>` to
  Checker, populated in `check_binary_op` when both bounds are coercible
  integer literals.
- Add `apply_deferred_range_bound_types` which runs after
  `default_unconstrained_range_types` in `check_program`.  It resolves
  each `TypeVar` to its concrete type, validates the literal fits (emitting
  an error if not), and re-records the bound span with the correct type.
- Add a `check_against` arm for `(Expr::Binary{Range|RangeInclusive},
  Range<T>)` so that range literals in typed positions (function args,
  `let r: Range<i32> = 0..10`) check the bounds directly against `T`.

Tests:
- `for_range_infers_i32_from_body_usage`: verifies bound spans are
  re-recorded as I32 (not I64) when body constrains the induction variable.
- `range_literal_check_against_range_i32`: range literal coerces to a
  `Range<i32>` parameter without errors.
- `for_range_unconstrained_defaults_to_i64`: unconstrained range still
  defaults to i64 (no regression).
- `for_range_bound_overflow_errors`: `0..300` with an i8 constraint now
  correctly errors instead of silently truncating.
- `range_literal_assigned_to_range_i32`: `let r: Range<i32> = 0..10`.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[analysis] General identifiability study: oblate, prolate, tri-axial, cylindrical

Comprehensive computational study testing identifiability lifting across
four shell geometries to support Paper 10's claim that geometry mediates
identifiability in fluid-filled viscoelastic shells.

Results:
- Oblate: kappa decreases from ~270 to ~88 with eccentricity (lifting confirmed)
- Prolate: kappa flat/worsening 334-688 (no lifting, as predicted by P9)
- Triaxial: non-monotonic 175-2595 (averaging effect from axis superposition)
- Cylinder: strong lifting, kappa drops from ~677 to ~73 with L/D (R^2=0.95)

Key finding: identifiability lifting is geometry-specific, not universal.
The oblate curvature-mode anti-correlation has no prolate analogue.
Cylindrical shells lift via axial/circumferential mode separation.

New files:
- scripts/general_identifiability.py (imports existing universality.py models,
  adds new capped-cylinder Donnell model, generates 2x2 panel figure)
- papers/paper10-capstone/figures/fig_general_identifiability.{pdf,png}

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[paper] P10: rigorous proof of forward-inverse adequacy gap theorem

Upgrade the forward-inverse adequacy observation from a numerical lemma to a
rigorous three-part theorem (Theorem 4) with full proof:

(i)   Structural rank gap: D(G∘π) has rank ≤ d < n by the chain rule,
      so the reduced model is structurally rank-deficient regardless of
      forward accuracy.

(ii)  Coexistence: forward adequacy (small ε_fwd) coexists with inverse
      inadequacy (κ = ∞ for reduced model) at the same operating point.
      The mechanism is the kernel of Dπ: the reduced model is exactly
      insensitive to directions the full model can detect.

(iii) Quantitative bound: κ(F) ≥ σ₁/ψ where ψ is the null-space
      sensitivity. In the near-spherical limit, ψ ~ μ₁α² giving
      κ ~ C/α², connecting to the existing Proposition 3 asymptotics.

Files added:
- papers/paper10-capstone/sections/theorem4_proof.tex (standalone LaTeX)
- scripts/forward_inverse_proof.py (numerical verification + figure)
- papers/paper10-capstone/figures/fig_forward_inverse_gap.{pdf,png}

Numerical verification at canonical point:
- rank(J_sphere) = 2 < 3 = rank(J_ritz)
- ℰ_fwd = 9.36% < 10% yet κ_sphere/κ_ritz = 1.97×10⁸
- ψ/σ₃ = 42 (null direction is not the globally weakest)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
